text:
Market today: The ECB is expected to hold rates, but hawkish hints could emerge, signaling tighter monetary policy. This is the main driver, as higher rates can pressure growth-oriented tech and chip names, especially when trading on margin.

Your holdings: SOXL is a "SELL" down 37.71%, and SPAL, a "NEW" position, is down a staggering 48.51%. Both are leveraged ETFs and decaying, amplifying losses. QQQI is a "TRIM" down 2.09%. Margin amplifies these drawdowns significantly.

Watch: Closely monitor the ECB's announcement for any hawkish language. Also, keep a tight watch on your leveraged SOXL and SPAL positions, ensuring stops are active and appropriate.

Bottom line: Aggressively manage risk on your leveraged holdings, as potential rate shifts could further impact your concentrated tech book.
plays: SOXL is a "SELL" and down 37.71%; your 200 stop is now a target. Re-evaluate this leveraged position and set a new, active stop to protect capital.
SPAL is "NEW" but down 48.51%; your 21.38 stop is now a target. This leveraged ETF needs immediate attention; consider cutting losses or setting a new stop.
Trim QQQI as indicated by the scanner; it's down 2.09% and close to its 52 stop.
